title: UCS Central Domain Registration and Heartbeat Health (multi-domain governance)
exclusions: This use case is not UC-19.1.14 (UCSM `mgmtBackup` / All Configuration backup validation on a per-domain UCS Manager poll). It is not UC-19.1.19 (Cisco Intersight SaaS `cond/Alarms` on `cisco:intersight:alarms`), UC-19.1.23 (Intersight audit on `cisco:intersight:aaaauditrecord`), or UC-19.1.33 (Intersight device connector and tunnel health on `cisco:intersight:assetdeviceregistration`). UCS Central is on-premises, speaks Cisco UCS Manager-style XML over HTTPS to `/nuova`, and is governed by `extpolClient` in Central’s class tree; Intersight is a separate cloud control plane with REST APIs and connector tunnels—similar words, different products. This UC does not cover UCS Central **global** service profile pool capacity or `compResource` pool exhaustion, does not monitor firmware **staging** policy drifts, and is not a substitute for per-domain `cisco:ucs:faults` or `cisco:ucs:events` health inside each UCSM when you need blade-level data-plane detail. It also does not replace manual certificate inventory in your PKI: it surfaces *symptoms* of trust or heartbeat loss, not a full X.509 lifecycle program.
dataSources:
**Index:** `cisco_ucs` (or a dedicated `cisco_ucs_central` if your governance team splits governance streams—keep one consistent name in `inputs.conf` and in every `index=` in this file). **Primary stream:** one JSON line per `extpolClient` per poll from a **custom** Heavy Forwarder scripted input that calls UCS Central’s **`/nuova`** XML API, posts **`aaaLogin`**, then **`configResolveClass`** for **`classId="extpolClient"`**, then **`aaaLogout`**, and sends the parsed rows to **HEC** with **`sourcetype=cisco:ucs_central:extpolclient`**. Example request body (XML) after login cookie is acquired:

`<configResolveClass inHierarchical="false" classId="extpolClient"/>`

**Fields you should flatten into JSON at index time (names may arrive camelCase; the SPL defensively `coalesce`s snake_case):** `name` (UCSM domain FQDN or label), `regState`, `lastHeartbeat` (human/epoch as Central returns), `lastHeartbeatTs` (if present), `connStatus`, `mode`, `siteName`, `tdId`, and `dn`. **Optional corroboration:** syslog from UCS Central (audit/fault) via SC4S or a syslog receiver, often mapped to `cisco:ucs:syslog` from the **Splunk_TA_cisco_ucs** parsing pack, or a dedicated `cisco:ucs_central:syslog` if you segregate. **Asymmetric trust (optional second project):** poll each UCSM domain for **`extpolReg`** to compare “this domain’s view of Central” against Central’s `extpolClient`—the saved search in this file focuses on Central-side truth first.
app: `Splunk_TA_cisco_ucs` — **Splunk Add-on for Cisco UCS** ([Splunkbase 2731](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2731)), supported on Splunk Enterprise 9.x / 10.x and Splunk Cloud. Install on Search Heads and forwarders that parse UCS syslog. **Important:** the stock add-on’s modular inputs target **UCS Manager** and publish sourcetypes such as `cisco:ucs`, `cisco:ucs:inventory`, `cisco:ucs:faults`, `cisco:ucs:events`, `cisco:ucs:stats`, `cisco:ucs:syslog`, and `cisco:ucs:audit`. There is **no** first-class, Splunkbase-shipped poller for **UCS Central** as a separate product, so you add the **custom** scripted input + HEC path this use case documents under `local/` in that TA (or a small companion app) on a **Heavy Forwarder** that can reach Central’s management VIP on `443/tcp`.
spl:
// UC-19.1.18 — UCS Central extpolClient registration and heartbeat health (one row per registered UCS Manager domain).
// Sourcetype cisco:ucs_central:extpolclient comes from the custom HEC poller in detailedImplementation; lookup ucs_central_domain_owners.csv carries business service + change-freeze.
index=cisco_ucs sourcetype="cisco:ucs_central:extpolclient" earliest=-4h@h
| eval domain_name=coalesce(name, domain_name, ucs_domain, "")
| eval reg_raw=coalesce(regState, reg_state, regstate, "")
| eval reg_s=lower(trim(reg_raw))
| eval last_hb=coalesce(lastHeartbeat, last_heartbeat, "")
| eval last_ts=coalesce(lastHeartbeatTs, last_heartbeat_ts, lastHbTs, "")
| eval site_l=coalesce(siteName, site_name, "")
| eval mode_l=coalesce(mode, reg_mode, "")
| eval conn_l=coalesce(connStatus, conn_status, "")
| eval hb_parse=if(len(last_ts)>0 AND last_ts!="0", coalesce(strptime(last_ts, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3N%z"), strptime(last_ts, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3N"), strptime(last_ts, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S")), null())
| eval hb_parse=if(isnull(hb_parse) AND match(last_hb, "^\\d+(\\.\\d+)?$"), tonumber(last_hb), hb_parse)
| eval hb_parse=if(isnull(hb_parse) AND len(last_hb)>0, coalesce(strptime(last_hb, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3N%z"), strptime(last_hb, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3N"), strptime(last_hb, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S")), hb_parse)
| eval heartbeat_age_seconds=if(isnotnull(hb_parse), now()-hb_parse, null())
| where domain_name!=""
| sort 0 - _time
| stats latest(_time) as _time, latest(reg_s) as reg_s, latest(heartbeat_age_seconds) as heartbeat_age_seconds, latest(last_hb) as last_hb, latest(last_ts) as last_ts, latest(site_l) as site_l, latest(mode_l) as mode_l, latest(conn_l) as conn_l by domain_name
| lookup ucs_central_domain_owners.csv domain_name OUTPUT domain_dn, business_service, owner_email, change_freeze_until
| eval freeze_active=if(isnotnull(change_freeze_until) AND len(change_freeze_until)>0 AND now()<coalesce(strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"), strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%d"), 0), 1, 0)
| eval severity_bucket=case(reg_s!="registered" OR reg_s="" OR isnull(reg_s),"REGISTRY_STATE",isnotnull(heartbeat_age_seconds) AND reg_s=="registered" AND heartbeat_age_seconds>300,"HEARTBEAT_STALE",isnotnull(heartbeat_age_seconds) AND reg_s=="registered" AND heartbeat_age_seconds>120,"HEARTBEAT_ELEVATED",1=1,"OK")
| eval severity_bucket=if(freeze_active=1 AND severity_bucket!="OK", "OK_FREEZE", severity_bucket)
| eval severity_order=case(severity_bucket=="REGISTRY_STATE",1,severity_bucket=="HEARTBEAT_STALE",2,severity_bucket=="HEARTBEAT_ELEVATED",3,severity_bucket=="OK_FREEZE",4,1=1,5)
| table _time, domain_name, domain_dn, reg_s, heartbeat_age_seconds, last_hb, last_ts, site_l, mode_l, conn_l, business_service, owner_email, change_freeze_until, freeze_active, severity_bucket, severity_order
| sort 0 severity_order, - heartbeat_age_seconds
description: Watches every UCS Central `**extpolClient**` row the poller materialises: each row is one registered UCS Manager domain as Central sees it, with `name`, `regState`, and heartbeat clocks (`lastHeartbeat`, `lastHeartbeatTs`), plus context `siteName`, `mode`, and `connStatus`. The SPL `coalesce`s both camelCase and snake_case in case the forwarder renames fields, derives `heartbeat_age_seconds` with `now() - strptime(...)` when timestamps are present, and assigns `**severity_bucket**`: any lifecycle state that is not `registered` (for example `unregistering`, `failed`, `inProgress`) is `**REGISTRY_STATE**`; for `registered` rows, a heartbeat age above 300s (Central normally refreshes roughly every 120s) is `HEARTBEAT_STALE`, and 120–300s is `HEARTBEAT_ELEVATED`. A `lookup` to `ucs_central_domain_owners.csv` adds service ownership and a change-freeze flag so pages respect maintenance. Optional cross-validation (not in the base `spl` but in detailedImplementation) joins `extpolReg` from each UCSM side to catch asymmetric trust where Central and a domain disagree after certificate or network events.
value: UCS Central is the **multi-domain** policy and inventory hub for a whole fleet: one instance may anchor 0–50 domains, so a silent registration or heartbeat loss can strand org-wide service profiles, pools, and ID pools while individual racks still look healthy. This use case gives a single-pane, audit-friendly read of *who* Central believes is in membership and *how fresh* the last check-in is, so certificate rotations, management-network incidents, and governance freezes surface before you chase phantom “pending” work. The evidence pack (saved search, lookup-enriched table, and the HEC poller) supports ITGC-style questions on change control to the control plane without mixing in unrelated backup or Intersight feeds.
implementation: Run the Python **/nuova** poller on a Heavy Forwarder, land JSON in `index=cisco_ucs` as `cisco:ucs_central:extpolclient`, deploy `ucs_central_domain_owners.csv` and `transforms.conf`, then schedule the `spl` every **15m** on `-4h@h` with throttling on `domain_name` and suppress during `change_freeze_until` (via lookup or a maintenance macro).
visualization: (1) **Column chart** of **`severity_order`** by **`business_service`** to show which teams still have elevated rows. (2) **Heatmap** of **`domain_name`** × hour with **`heartbeat_age_seconds`** (colour scale: green <120s, amber 120–300s, red >300s or `REGISTRY_STATE`). (3) **Pivot** or **table** of **`reg_s`** with counts and latest **`connStatus`**. (4) **Single value**: number of rows where **`severity_bucket`≠"OK`**. (5) Optional overlay panel from `cisco:ucs:syslog` filtered to Central hostname for human-readable fault strings next to the numeric panel.

references.title: Cisco UCS Central Software Configuration Guide (support hub)
Cisco UCS Manager XML API — configResolveClass and managed objects
Send data to Splunk HTTP Event Collector (HEC)

knownFalsePositives: A planned **UCS Central HA** failover (active/standby) can move sessions and produce short `regState` transitions or heartbeat gaps; page only if the condition persists past your HA completion window. **Certificate trust-store rotation** for Central↔UCSM often causes 5–15 minutes of `inProgress` or `failed` states that self-heal when new trust material is in place—use your PKI change ticket to suppress. A chassis or full FI power cycle in a domain during maintenance can pause heartbeats even though the eventual state is fine; join to your hardware change schedule. NTP skew between Central and a UCSM domain can make `heartbeat_age_seconds` look worse than reality—check `ntpd` or `chrony` on both ends before a sev-1. A management VLAN MTU or ACL change can delay XML replies without changing `regState` until timeouts accumulate. Decommission flows that intentionally `unregister` a domain are expected: mark those domains in `ucs_central_domain_owners.csv` with an end-of-life date or route them to a `DECOM` service line so the alert does not fire forever. Capacity-only work that never touches management connectivity should not need this control—if it fires, the event is still often real, but your storage network team is the wrong first call: validate management reachability first.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• **Splunk_TA_cisco_ucs** ([2731](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2731)) on Search Heads (for `cisco:ucs:syslog` knowledge if you add syslog) and on the **Heavy Forwarder** that will host the poller, even though Central itself is not a stock modular input. Index `cisco_ucs` (or `cisco_ucs_central`) created with your standard retention; role `srchIndexesAllowed` must include that index for operators running the search.
• **Network path:** the forwarder must reach `https://<ucs-central-vip-or-dns>/nuova` on `443/tcp` and trust the **TLS** chain presented by Central; if you use an internal CA, place the CA bundle in the poller’s environment or the OS trust store. Service account on Central with at least read-only `aaa` and `config` scope to resolve `extpolClient` (follow least privilege your SecOps team approves for automation).
• **HEC** token on the indexer (or a dedicated HEC on the HF if you have an intermediate **HEC ack** design); prefer TLS HEC. Document the token in **Secrets** storage, not in git.
• **Lookup** file `ucs_central_domain_owners.csv` in `$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/searchlookup/` (or the app that owns the saved search) with at least: `domain_name, domain_dn, business_service, owner_email, change_freeze_until` where `change_freeze_until` is a timestamp or empty. Use `change_freeze_until` in alert suppression in Step 4.
• **Baseline expectation:** a healthy `extpolClient` with `regState=registered` should see a heartbeat field update on a **~120s** cadence; treat **>300s** as actionable drift when the state still claims `registered`.

Step 1 — Configure data collection (custom HEC poller + parsing)

1A. HEC token (indexer or HF listening for HEC). Example *Indexer* `inputs.conf` fragment (path varies by your layout):

```ini
# $SPLUNK_HOME/etc/system/local/inputs.conf  (or an app on the indexer that owns HEC)
[http://ucs_central_governance]
disabled = 0
token = <YOUR_HEC_TOKEN>
indexes = cisco_ucs
index = cisco_ucs
sourcetype = manual
```

1B. Scripted input on the **Heavy Forwarder** that both polls `/nuova` and **POSTs** JSON lines to HEC. Place the script under the TA or a child app, for example: `$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/Splunk_TA_cisco_ucs/bin/ucs_central_extpolclient_poll.py`.

# $SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/Splunk_TA_Cisco_UCS/local/inputs.conf  (on Linux the folder may be lowercase; match your on-disk name)

```ini
[script://$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/Splunk_TA_cisco_ucs/bin/ucs_central_extpolclient_poll.py]
interval = 120
disabled = 0
sourcetype = cisco:ucs_central:extpolclient
index = cisco_ucs
host = ucs_central_nuova_poller
passAuth = false
```

1C. **props.conf** — force JSON for the sourcetype (your script emits one JSON per line as the *raw* HEC *event* body):

# $SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/Splunk_TA_Cisco_UCS/local/props.conf  (on-disk case may differ)

```ini
[cisco:ucs_central:extpolclient]
TRUNCATE = 1048576
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
KV_MODE = json
category = Application
LOOKUP-ucs_central_owners = ucs_central_domain_owners domain_name AS domain_name OUTPUT domain_dn, business_service, owner_email, change_freeze_until
```

(You may omit the `LOOKUP-` line if you prefer lookup only at search time; the saved SPL already calls `lookup` explicitly.)

1D. **transforms.conf** (lookup table registration):

# $SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/Splunk_TA_Cisco_UCS/local/transforms.conf  (on-disk case may differ)

```ini
[ucs_central_domain_owners]
filename = ucs_central_domain_owners.csv
case_sensitive_match = false
min_matches = 1
default_match = -
```

1E. **Python** scripted input — polls `extpolClient` and posts JSON to HEC. The script is intentionally self-contained: adjust `UCS_CENTRAL_BASE`, `UCS_USER`, `UCS_PASSWORD` via environment variables or a protected `.conf` the script reads from `/opt/splunk/secure/ucs_central_poller.env` (not shown) — never hard-code production secrets in this repository.

```python
#!/opt/splunk/bin/python3
import json, os, ssl, sys, time, urllib.error, urllib.request, xml.etree.ElementTree as ET

# --- CONFIG (override with environment variables) ---
UCS = os.environ.get("UCS_CENTRAL_BASE", "https://ucs-central.example.com/nuova").rstrip("/") + "/"
USER = os.environ.get("UCS_CENTRAL_USER", "ucspoll")
PW = os.environ.get("UCS_CENTRAL_PASSWORD", "change-me")
HEC = os.environ.get("SPLUNK_HEC_URL", "https://indexer:8088/services/collector/event")
HEC_TOKEN = os.environ.get("SPLUNK_HEC_TOKEN", "")
SVC_INSECURE = os.environ.get("UCS_INSECURE_TLS", "0") == "1"  # lab only
# ---

def _localname(tag):
    return tag.rsplit("}", 1)[-1] if "}" in tag else tag

def _post(url, body, headers=None, ctx=None):
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, data=body.encode("utf-8"), method="POST")
    for k, v in (headers or {}).items():
        req.add_header(k, v)
    return urllib.request.urlopen(req, context=ctx, timeout=90).read()

def _ssl():
    if SVC_INSECURE:
        return ssl._create_unverified_context()
    return ssl.create_default_context()

def _xml_call(xml_body, ctx):
    return _post(UCS, xml_body, {"Content-Type": "application/xml"}, ctx)

def _parse_login_cookie(xml_bytes):
    root = ET.fromstring(xml_bytes)
    for elem in root.iter():
        if _localname(elem.tag).lower() == "aaalogin" and ("outCookie" in elem.attrib or "cookie" in elem.attrib):
            return elem.attrib.get("outCookie") or elem.attrib.get("cookie")
    return None

def _collect_extpolClients(cookie, ctx):
    q = f'<configResolveClass cookie="{cookie}" inHierarchical="false" classId="extpolClient"/>'
    resp = _xml_call(q, ctx)
    root = ET.fromstring(resp)
    return [dict(el.attrib) for el in root.iter() if _localname(el.tag) == "extpolClient" and el.attrib]

def _hec_send(row, ctx_http):
    hec = {
        "time": int(time.time()),
        "host": os.environ.get("SPLUNK_HEC_HOST", "ucs_central"),
        "source": os.environ.get("SPLUNK_HEC_SOURCE", "ucs_central:extpolclient"),
        "sourcetype": "cisco:ucs_central:extpolclient",
        "index": os.environ.get("SPLUNK_INDEX", "cisco_ucs"),
        "event": row,
    }
    payload = (json.dumps(hec) + "\n").encode("utf-8")
    _post(
        HEC,
        payload,
        {"Authorization": f"Splunk {HEC_TOKEN}", "Content-Type": "application/json; profile=splunk_event"},
        ctx_http,
    )

def main():
    ctx = _ssl()
    hctx = _ssl() if HEC.startswith("https") else None
    last_err = None
    for attempt in range(1, 4):
        try:
            r_login = _xml_call(f'<aaaLogin inName="{USER}" inPassword="{PW}"/>', ctx)
            cookie = _parse_login_cookie(r_login)
            if not cookie:
                raise RuntimeError("aaaLogin: no outCookie in response")
            clients = _collect_extpolClients(cookie, ctx)
            try:
                _xml_call(f'<aaaLogout inCookie="{cookie}"/>', ctx)
            except (urllib.error.URLError, OSError) as le:
                print(f"aaaLogout: {le}", file=sys.stderr)
            for row in clients:
                _hec_send(row, hctx)
            return 0
        except (urllib.error.URLError, ssl.SSLError, ET.ParseError, RuntimeError) as e:
            last_err = e
            time.sleep(min(5 * attempt, 20))
    print(f"FATAL: {last_err!r}", file=sys.stderr)
    return 1

if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(main())
```

Hardening notes: run the poller with a read-only user; in production, mount **Splunk**’s HEC with TLS and a token from **Password** storage, not inline. If Central returns more than one XML namespace, the `iter()` path above still ingests any element whose local name is `extpolClient`.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

The saved search (copy exactly into a report or alert) is:

```spl
// UC-19.1.18 — UCS Central extpolClient registration and heartbeat health (one row per registered UCS Manager domain).
// Sourcetype cisco:ucs_central:extpolclient comes from the custom HEC poller in detailedImplementation; lookup ucs_central_domain_owners.csv carries business service + change-freeze.
index=cisco_ucs sourcetype="cisco:ucs_central:extpolclient" earliest=-4h@h
| eval domain_name=coalesce(name, domain_name, ucs_domain, "")
| eval reg_raw=coalesce(regState, reg_state, regstate, "")
| eval reg_s=lower(trim(reg_raw))
| eval last_hb=coalesce(lastHeartbeat, last_heartbeat, "")
| eval last_ts=coalesce(lastHeartbeatTs, last_heartbeat_ts, lastHbTs, "")
| eval site_l=coalesce(siteName, site_name, "")
| eval mode_l=coalesce(mode, reg_mode, "")
| eval conn_l=coalesce(connStatus, conn_status, "")
| eval hb_parse=if(len(last_ts)>0 AND last_ts!="0", coalesce(strptime(last_ts, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3N%z"), strptime(last_ts, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3N"), strptime(last_ts, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S")), null())
| eval hb_parse=if(isnull(hb_parse) AND match(last_hb, "^\\d+(\\.\\d+)?$"), tonumber(last_hb), hb_parse)
| eval hb_parse=if(isnull(hb_parse) AND len(last_hb)>0, coalesce(strptime(last_hb, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3N%z"), strptime(last_hb, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3N"), strptime(last_hb, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S")), hb_parse)
| eval heartbeat_age_seconds=if(isnotnull(hb_parse), now()-hb_parse, null())
| where domain_name!=""
| sort 0 - _time
| stats latest(_time) as _time, latest(reg_s) as reg_s, latest(heartbeat_age_seconds) as heartbeat_age_seconds, latest(last_hb) as last_hb, latest(last_ts) as last_ts, latest(site_l) as site_l, latest(mode_l) as mode_l, latest(conn_l) as conn_l by domain_name
| lookup ucs_central_domain_owners.csv domain_name OUTPUT domain_dn, business_service, owner_email, change_freeze_until
| eval freeze_active=if(isnotnull(change_freeze_until) AND len(change_freeze_until)>0 AND now()<coalesce(strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"), strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%d"), 0), 1, 0)
| eval severity_bucket=case(reg_s!="registered" OR reg_s="" OR isnull(reg_s),"REGISTRY_STATE",isnotnull(heartbeat_age_seconds) AND reg_s=="registered" AND heartbeat_age_seconds>300,"HEARTBEAT_STALE",isnotnull(heartbeat_age_seconds) AND reg_s=="registered" AND heartbeat_age_seconds>120,"HEARTBEAT_ELEVATED",1=1,"OK")
| eval severity_bucket=if(freeze_active=1 AND severity_bucket!="OK", "OK_FREEZE", severity_bucket)
| eval severity_order=case(severity_bucket=="REGISTRY_STATE",1,severity_bucket=="HEARTBEAT_STALE",2,severity_bucket=="HEARTBEAT_ELEVATED",3,severity_bucket=="OK_FREEZE",4,1=1,5)
| table _time, domain_name, domain_dn, reg_s, heartbeat_age_seconds, last_hb, last_ts, site_l, mode_l, conn_l, business_service, owner_email, change_freeze_until, freeze_active, severity_bucket, severity_order
| sort 0 severity_order, - heartbeat_age_seconds
```

**Alerting:** `cron */15 * * * *` over `-4h@h`, **trigger if** `severity_bucket` in (`REGISTRY_STATE`, `HEARTBEAT_STALE`) after `| where severity_order<=2` (or equivalent) and **throttle** 60–120 minutes by `domain_name`, unless `now() < strptime(change_freeze_until, ...)` in a follow-on eval that suppresses known maintenance.

**Asymmetric check (separate report):** if you add `sourcetype` from UCSM-side `extpolReg` to the same or another index, join on a normalised FQDN and compare; mismatch between Central and UCSM after a **certificate** event is a high-priority item for your **PKI** and **UCS** platform teams together.

Step 3 — Validate
1. In Splunk, run a simple raw check: `index=cisco_ucs sourcetype="cisco:ucs_central:extpolclient" | head 20` and confirm you see the fields `name`/`regState`/`lastHeartbeat*`. If the JSON is nested, add `spath` in a one-off, then fix the poller to flatten.
2. On Central’s CLI or GUI, compare one domain that must be `registered` and verify `lastHeartbeat` is moving. Your computed `heartbeat_age_seconds` should be **<120s** under steady state.
3. Inject a test: create a read-only break on the management path to one lab domain (or use a read-only `iptables` in lab) and confirm **`HEARTBEAT_STALE`** appears before any service-profile work is attempted.
4. Confirm the lookup: `| inputlookup ucs_central_domain_owners.csv | head` from the Search app.
5. If syslog is also present, correlate one failing domain’s window with any `%UCS*` or Central-specific message around the same time — but the numeric `spl` remains authoritative for governance.

Step 4 — Operationalise
Add the dashboard row described in the `visualization` field: heatmap, service bars, and a single value for not-`OK` rows. Wire **ServiceNow** or **Jira** with a payload that includes `domain_name`, `reg_s`, `heartbeat_age_seconds`, `owner_email`, and a deep link to the Central **Domains** view. Rehearse quarterly with your UCS platform team: walk through **HA failover**, **cert rotation**, and a controlled **unregister** in lab so runbooks match reality. Keep the lookup fresh when domains are **added or retired**; stale rows cause ownership mistakes, not data-plane outages.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
Empty index: the scripted input is not running (`splunkd.log` for `ExecProcessor`), the HEC token is rejected (`401` on HEC), or Central is blocking the service account. Wrong ages: NTP or mixed timestamp formats; extend `strptime` in the `eval hb_parse` chain. Constant `HEARTBEAT_ELEVATED` for all rows: the poller is ingesting a **static** copy of the XML; verify you are not reading a **cached** file. Partial domains missing: the API user lacks scope for some org, or a domain truly **unregistered** — compare Central UI. HEC event too large: split batched children per domain (preferred one event per `extpolClient` as shown). **TLS** errors: your Central chain is not trusted on the forwarder. **Alert fatigue:** tighten from `ELEVATED` to only `STALE`+`REGISTRY` after baseline weeks.
grandmaExplanation: We check that each site your central place still shows on the list and has checked in lately. If one drifts or falls off, shared rules and updates can stop partway, and the two sides can disagree after trust work. We help you see that before people chase a change that never fully landed.
hardware: Cisco UCS Central 1.x, 2.x, and 2.5.x (VMware-virtualised) as the XML API target; each managed UCS Manager domain is typically 6x00-series Fabric Interconnects (6248UP, 6296UP, 6332, 6332-16UP, 6454, 6536) in switch mode, with X-Series and Intersight-adjacent designs sometimes using IMM-mode FIs—those domains still show up in `extpolClient` when registered to Central, but the physical NIC path differs, so your management-network checks must be IMM-aware. Deploy Central as a single VM for labs or a separate-ESXi-node HA pair in production; heartbeat expectations assume Central’s own clock and service health are good. Harden 443/tcp from the Heavy Forwarder to the Central VIP, not the wildcard internet.
